summary: This 2017 post surveys the data warehouse automation (DWA) tool landscape, comparing market presence using Google Trends data. Späti distinguishes between model-driven approaches (design first, then find data) and data-driven approaches (generate models from available data). He references external resources including Hans Michiels' comprehensive comparison guide and survey results. The post notes that most vendors lack transparent pricing, with BimlFlex being a notable exception. WhereScape and TimeXtender lead in search volume, though the author acknowledges search data conflates company and product names.
key_points: WhereScape and TimeXtender dominate DWA tool searches, though results are skewed by company name overlap
Model-driven DWA ensures business requirements are documented upfront; data-driven suits agile methodology better
Most DWA vendors hide pricing, requiring custom quotes - BimlFlex stands out for transparent pricing
Data-driven approach lets developers show real results quickly, aligning with agile practices
The DWA market includes tools ranging from open-source to expensive enterprise solutions with varying features
money_quotes: You quickly generate a model with the data which is available
The benefit is you only work with data that is actually there and the business has already a real-life model that represents the data
This suits very well with the agile methodology of Data Warehouse Automation tools
One of the only ones who provide transparent pricing is BimlFlex
Other vendors are not transparent and hand over prices on request
key_insight: Data-driven warehouse automation aligns well with agile methodology because it lets teams work with actual data and show real results to stakeholders quickly.
